evidence: Docstring asks whether some t >= 1 makes the Ahmes series sum 1/(a_n+t) irrational (disproven by Kovac and Tao).
Docstring: 'Let F_1=F_2=1 and F_{n+1} = F_n + F_{n-1} be the Fibonacci sequence' — the problem is about sums of reciprocal Fibonacci numbers.
Docstring asks 'Must sum_k 1/F_{n_k} be irrational?' (erdosproblems tag: irrationality).
Docstring: 'The sum sum_n 1/F_n itself was proved to be irrational by Andre-Jeannin' — that sum is the reciprocal Fibonacci constant.
The statement concerns the set of values of Ahmes series sum_{n in A} 1/(n+i), i.e. sums of unit fractions over an infinite set A (KoTa24 irrationality-problems paper).
Docstring asks whether sum_n 1/[a_1,...,a_n] is rational, where [a_1,...,a_n] is the least common multiple of the sequence prefixes.
Docstring asks whether sum_n 1/[a_1,...,a_n] is irrational, with [a_1,...,a_n] the partial least common multiples.
Docstring asks 'Is the sum ... irrational?' (erdosproblems tag: irrationality).
Docstring: 'the case where the set of primes P is infinite. In this case the sum is irrational.'
Docstring asks for the largest t with A_i cap A_j 'a non-empty arithmetic progression for all i != j' (erdosproblems tags: arithmetic progressions).
Docstring: 'Is there a covering system all of whose moduli are of the form p-1 for some primes p >= 5?' (erdosproblems tag: covering systems).
Docstring: 'Is there a covering system all of whose moduli are of the form p-1 for some primes p >= 3?' — solved variant of the covering-system question.

Docstring: 'Herzog and Schonheim conjectured that if A forms a partition of G with k > 1, then the indices [G:G_1],...,[G:G_k] cannot be distinct'; file header links the Wikipedia Herzog–Schonheim conjecture page.
erdosproblems.com/274 is annotated 'Herzog-Schonheim conjecture'; this decl states the exact-coset-covering variant with equal cardinalities rather than equal indices.
Solved finite-abelian special case of the exact-coset-covering (Herzog–Schonheim) question stated in this file.
Docstring: 'If a finite system of r congruences ... covers 2^r consecutive integers then it covers all integers' (Crittenden–Vanden Eynden; erdosproblems tag: covering systems).
Reference [CrVE70] is titled 'Any n arithmetic progressions covering the first 2^n integers cover all integers' — the congruence classes are arithmetic progressions.
Docstring: 'Is there an infinite Lucas sequence a_0, a_1, ... where a_{n+2} = a_{n+1} + a_n ...' — the problem is about Lucas (Fibonacci-like) sequences.
Docstring requires 'all a_k are composite' — compositeness of every term is the core condition.
A Fibonacci-like sequence with all terms composite is a primefree sequence; the docstring adds the coprimality condition 'no integer has a common factor with every term'.
